title: symphyotrichum bracteolatum
intro: Symphyotrichum bracteolatum, also known as the variegated aster or variegated American-aster, is a plant species in the daisy family, characterized by its showy flowers and distinctive variegated foliage. The medicinal properties of Symphyotrichum bracteolatum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and antispasmodic properties, providing relief from congestion and coughs. Herbal preparations of Symphyotrichum bracteolatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to make syrups and salves for topical application.
